title: Work and Travel in Canada with International Experience Canada - Canada.ca
content:
## Work and Travel in Canada with International Experience Canada
If you're 18 to 35 (18 to 30 in some countries), come work and travel in Canada through International Experience Canada (IEC)! It's a government program designed to help you
- gain valuable international work experience
- improve your language skills in English and French
- work in Canada and fund the trip of a lifetime
Are you Canadian? Learn how you can work and travel abroad with IEC .
See eligibility by country and category .
Create your IEC profile and get your invitation to apply
Submit your work permit application
Learn more about IEC
The pools for the 2026 season are open. If you’re eligible, you can create a profile .
Check the rounds of invitations for key dates and available spots.

### If you’re in Canada with a valid letter of introduction (LOI)
- You may be able to get your IEC work permit mailed to a Canadian address if you meet certain requirements.
- If you don’t meet these requirements, you must leave Canada after you get your port of entry LOI and re-enter from a country other than the US or Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon to activate your work permit.
CAN Error loading fee. Check the fee list .
Additional fees may apply.
Current processing time
Error loading the number of days. Try again.
after you submit all documents and give biometrics
Recognized organizations (ROs)
ROs may be able to help you plan your experience.

## Services and information

### Rounds of invitations
Find out about important dates, the number of invitations sent out and the number of spots available.

### Make
            changes to your IEC work permit
Find out how to make changes to your work permit in specific situations.

### Check
            the status of your application
Get online updates about your application.

### IEC
            web form
Complete the web form if you need to submit more documents or if you need more time to submit your documents.

### Find a job in Canada
Search the government's Job Bank for job opportunities across the country.

### Get answers to
            your questions
Get answers to frequently asked questions about IEC in the Help Centre.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
